---
title: "Game of Thrones Season 8 Viewership Analysis"
author: "Weidong Bai"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE
)

library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
```

# Overview

*Game of Thrones* is an American fantasy drama television series created by David Benioff and D. B. Weiss for HBO. It is based on the fantasy novel series *A Song of Ice and Fire* by George R. R. Martin. The eighth and final season premiered in 2019 and concluded the story of the battle for the Iron Throne.

# Show Logo

```{r}
knitr::include_graphics("got_logo.jpg")
```

# Data Import

```{r}
got <- read.csv("season_8.csv")
```

got


# Summary Statistics

```{r}
summary_stats <- data.frame(
  Mean_Viewers = mean(got$viewers),
  Median_Viewers = median(got$viewers),
  Max_Viewers = max(got$viewers),
  Min_Viewers = min(got$viewers),
  SD_Viewers = sd(got$viewers)
)

kable(summary_stats, digits = 2) %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE, bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"))
```

The average episode attracted **`r round(mean(got$viewers),2)` million viewers**.  
The most watched episode had **`r round(max(got$viewers),2)` million viewers**, while the least watched had **`r round(min(got$viewers),2)` million viewers**.

# Viewership Over Time

```{r}
ggplot(got, aes(x = no_season, y = viewers)) +
  geom_line(color = "#1f77b4", linewidth = 1.5) +
  geom_point(color = "#d62728", size = 4) +
  labs(
    title = "Viewership Over Time",
    x = "Episode Number",
    y = "Viewers (Millions)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
```

# Episode-to-Episode Changes

```{r}
got$viewership_change <- c(NA, diff(got$viewers))

ggplot(got[-1, ], aes(x = no_season, y = viewership_change)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#2ca02c") +
  labs(
    title = "Episode-to-Episode Changes in Viewership",
    x = "Episode Number",
    y = "Viewer Change (Millions)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
```

# Analysis of Observed Changes

```{r}
max_increase <- max(got$viewership_change, na.rm = TRUE)
max_decrease <- min(got$viewership_change, na.rm = TRUE)

increase_episode <- got$no_season[which.max(got$viewership_change)]
decrease_episode <- got$no_season[which.min(got$viewership_change)]
```

The largest increase in audience occurred before **Episode `r increase_episode`**, with viewership rising by **`r round(max_increase,2)` million**.

The largest decrease occurred before **Episode `r decrease_episode`**, with viewership falling by **`r abs(round(max_decrease,2))` million**.

Overall, the season demonstrated fluctuating audience engagement, with viewership peaking toward the final episodes.
